svg.text: THE INDEPENDENT GUIDE TO IBM-COMPATIBLE PERSONAL COMPUTERS
▸ polyDBackup — Verified Database Backups to S3
▸ sheLLaMa — AI Agentic Shell for Bash & PowerShell
▸ DHCP Bridge Testing — Debug VLANs in Seconds
▸ lsb_release — The RHEL 9 Fix You Need

g.text: 🤖 Backup complete. Ready to restore on new machine.

svg.text: The personal computer that runs both CP/M and MS-DOS.

text.tspan: Run your existing CP/M-80 software on the built-in Z80 processor —
then switch to MS-DOS for the latest business applications.
256KB RAM standard, expandable to 896KB. Dual 400KB floppies.
Optional 10MB Winchester. Full networking with DECnet.
PROCESSOR:
MEMORY:
DISPLAY:
12" green phosphor
STORAGE:
Dual 400KB 5¼"
HARD DISK:
10MB optional
NETWORK:
DECnet Ethernet
PORTS:
2 serial, 1 parallel
PRICE:
From $2,595

svg.text: Circle number 1 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: M
oving to a new Windows PC has always meant a lost
weekend. PCmigrate fixes that. One click exports your
entire environment — installed software with versions,
license keys, winget packages, WSL distributions (as
.tar or .vhdx), and AppData folders — to an external
drive. On the new machine, run the generated restore
script and 'winget import' and 'wsl --import' bring
everything back. Our test: 47 apps, 2 WSL distros,
under 5 minutes to export, 11 to restore.
The dark WPF GUI is polished and responsive. WSL-
Only mode makes it a standalone backup tool. The
interactive HTML checklist with per-app download links
and hidden license keys is a thoughtful touch.
Export produces a CSV inventory, HTML checklist,
winget JSON, WSL archives (.tar for WSL 1, .vhdx for
WSL 2), zipped AppData, and a self-contained restore
script. The "Export + Bundle" option wraps everything
into a single zip you can hand to anyone.
SmartScreen warnings on the unsigned installer are
the only friction. PowerShell 5.1 compatible. Works on
Windows 10 (1709+) and 11. Handles drive roots,
trailing slashes, and Zone.Identifier blocking gracefully.
Highly recommended for anyone upgrading their PC
or anyone who lives in WSL and wants a safety net.

svg.text: Circle number 203 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: D
atabase backups are like insurance: boring until you
need them. polyDBackup makes them reliable. This
shell-based tool dumps PostgreSQL, MySQL, or Maria-
DB to S3 with compression, optional AES-256 encryp-
tion, and automated restore verification via table-level
checksums. A hard-coded staging database prevents
accidental writes to production. The backup user gets
read-only grants on live data and full control only on
the staging workspace. Even a misconfigured cron job
can't damage live data.
We tested with a 40 GB PostgreSQL instance: dump,
compress, encrypt, upload, and verify in under 12
minutes. The '.verified' marker in S3 after a successful
checksum comparison gives peace of mind no other
backup tool provides out of the box. Retention purges
old backups automatically. Runs as a container, systemd
timer, or cron. Deducting one star only because docs
assume S3 CLI familiarity. Otherwise excellent for any
shop running PostgreSQL or MariaDB in production.

svg.text: Circle number 204 on the reader service card.

g.text: "The most talked about software in the industry" — BYTE
Compiles 27,000 lines/min. Integrated editor. Built-in debugger. Overlays. 8087 support. Only $99.95!
Call 1-800-255-8008 or see your dealer today.
Circle number 87 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: W
hat if your shell could write its own commands?
sheLLaMa integrates LLMs directly into bash and
PowerShell. Type a natural-language request prefixed
with a comma, and the AI proposes commands, executes
them (with approval), reads output, and iterates up to
10 rounds. Supports local LLMs via Ollama, cloud fall-
back, and distributed backends. Runs fully offline after
model pull. Permission tiers block destructive commands
while read-only ops auto-execute.
Beyond the shell: web GUI, REST API, OpenAI-compat-
ible endpoint (works with Cursor), image generation,
cloud cost estimation. Ambitious but cohesive.
We tested with Ollama + Llama 3 on RTX 4090.
Response times: 1.2s simple, 4-6s for agentic loops.
Conversation save/load, branching, and auto-compac-
tion are genuinely useful. The PowerShell integration is
equally capable — rare for AI tools that treat Windows
as an afterthought. File editing uses diff-based search-
and-replace with previews. Web search lets the AI look
up docs mid-task. Admin console shows token stats,
costs, backend health. Half star off for setup complexity.

svg.text: Circle number 205 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: Red Hat dropped lsb_release from RHEL 9, breaking
countless scripts. This drop-in shell replacement parses
/etc/os-release and provides identical output. Install it,
your automation works again. No dependencies beyond
bash. Dead simple, exactly as it should be.

svg.text: Circle number 206 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: Testing DHCP on bridged or VLAN-tagged interfaces is
painful. This script takes a MAC and bridge name, fires
a DHCP request, shows what happened. TUI mode
(--tui) gives split-screen real-time packet capture.
Invaluable for libvirt/KVM network debugging.

svg.text: Circle number 207 on the reader service card.

g.text: Circle number 45 on the reader service card.
Peter Norton Computing • Santa Monica, CA
Circle number 92 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: After reading your preview of PCmigrate, I set it up for WSL-only backups. Last week I ran 'rm -rf /' inside my
Ubuntu instance instead of the container I was targeting. PCmigrate had a 3-hour-old VHDX on my USB. Restored
in two minutes. — D. Markham, Portland, OR
We've run polyDBackup for three months. The verification step flagged a checksum mismatch — turned out a bad
RAM stick was flipping bits during dumps. Without checksums, we'd have restored corrupted backups and never
known. The staging safety model gives our DBA peace of mind. — R. Vasquez, Miami, FL
I was using Copilot CLI, a separate Ollama wrapper, and ChatGPT in a browser. sheLLaMa replaced all three. The
agentic loop is genuinely useful for sysadmin tasks, and it runs fully local. No data leaves my network.
— K. Johansson, Stockholm, Sweden

svg.text: 8-BIT ISA ETHERNET FOR EVERY PC IN YOUR OFFICE.

text.tspan: Works in any IBM PC, XT, AT, or compatible with a single 8-bit slot.
10Base-2 (thin coax) and AUI connectors. On-board 8KB buffer.
Hardware-configurable IRQ and I/O base address. Novell NetWare certified.
Includes drivers for NetWare 2.x/3.x, NDIS, and Packet Driver spec.
Full 802.3 compliance. 5-year limited warranty.
